acceptance_targets: [craft_001_replayed_public_choice] After the deadline is stated at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:295 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, the revised late-room scene must introduce at least one materially new conflict beat: a named loss, a forced crew choice, or an irreversible tactical concession visible on the page. If the room mainly restates heir doctrine and chooses Daro again in near-identical terms, fail.

[craft_002_sanitized_devotion_desire] In the revised version of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243, the passage must include at least one precise bodily response in Daro and one observable social consequence of his letting the touch linger. Abstract labels such as 'power,' 'plain body,' or generalized 'heat' without a concrete manifestation do not pass.

[prose_001_as_if_scaffold_tic] Across the cited lines, no more than two 'as if' comparison frames remain in the revised chapter, and each replacement must still deliver a concrete image or pressure effect that is readable on first pass.

[FBR-001] Pass if reading snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6655-6758 shows a messier yard with multiple concrete reasons for choosing sides, and if the split feels socially contingent rather than instantly doctrinal. Fail if the scene still resolves mainly through the phrase 'one saint, one body' and then snaps into tidy factions.

[FBR-012] Pass if reading snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6707-6710 lets the reader see the crowd shift without the sentence 'That landed.' Fail if the moment still depends on the formula or another near-identical impact placeholder.

findings.acceptance_test: After the deadline is stated at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:295 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, the revised late-room scene must introduce at least one materially new conflict beat: a named loss, a forced crew choice, or an irreversible tactical concession visible on the page. If the room mainly restates heir doctrine and chooses Daro again in near-identical terms, fail.
findings.problem: The chapter already turns Daro's claim into public doctrine on the porch and shows the yard splitting around it, but the late-night house scene largely asks the crowd to make the same ideological choice again. The deadline revelation is strong, yet too much of the late-room material replays an argument whose outcome the chapter has already established, which softens the launch beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the deadline material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:295 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, but re-engineer the late-room section around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:347, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:395 so it produces a new form of consequence instead of another crowd reconversion. Options: make the deadline force a concrete sacrifice, an irreversible logistical split, or a public abandonment that the porch scene could not already supply.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised version of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243, the passage must include at least one precise bodily response in Daro and one observable social consequence of his letting the touch linger. Abstract labels such as 'power,' 'plain body,' or generalized 'heat' without a concrete manifestation do not pass.
findings.problem: When the house turns Daro into a chosen body, the prose identifies the fusion of devotion, power, and desire but then retreats into euphemism: 'Some of it was power. Some of it was plain body' and 'the heat of attention settling low in him.' In a novel whose style bible explicitly asks for the concrete collision of sanctity and lust, this is sanitization drift at the exact point where the bodily cost of belief should become specific and uncomfortable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the petition sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243 so the eel-oil touch and Daro's response are rendered with exact bodily and social consequence. Keep the public setting, but replace the abstract summary with what the contact does to his throat, breath, posture, arousal, shame, or self-control, and show what the waiting room can or cannot read from him.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the cited lines, no more than two 'as if' comparison frames remain in the revised chapter, and each replacement must still deliver a concrete image or pressure effect that is readable on first pass.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps returning to the same 'as if' comparison scaffold for emphasis. Because it appears across all three scenes, the device stops feeling chosen and starts sounding automatic, flattening otherwise strong images into a repeated sentence habit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the recurring comparison frames at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:125, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:139, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:241, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:337, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:365, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:389, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:401. Preserve the pressure in those beats, but vary the syntax by using direct statement, different metaphor structures, or sharper concrete description instead of the same 'as if' hinge.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if reading snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6655-6758 shows a messier yard with multiple concrete reasons for choosing sides, and if the split feels socially contingent rather than instantly doctrinal. Fail if the scene still resolves mainly through the phrase 'one saint, one body' and then snaps into tidy factions.
findings.problem: The public schism sorts the community too quickly into a slogan and then into two neat camps. At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6659-6671 the chant crystallizes almost instantly, and at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6751-6755 the yard is summarized as saint-hungry believers versus work-hardened realists. That loses the novel's best social intelligence right when communal complexity should peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6655-6671, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6691-6709, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6751-6758 so allegiance shifts through concrete material pressures rather than a single chant. Give the split at least three distinct motives on the page: people who choose Daro because they cannot spare more labor, people who choose Riva because Marrow Market already taught them what shortcut ritual does, and people who move back and forth based on whose child or debt is immediately at risk. Let some practical workers cling to Daro and some pilgrims cross to Riva for ugly reasons.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if reading snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6707-6710 lets the reader see the crowd shift without the sentence 'That landed.' Fail if the moment still depends on the formula or another near-identical impact placeholder.
findings.problem: Full-book style pattern: the standalone 'That landed' impact beat appears in 10 chapters. In this chapter it appears at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6709 during the already crowded public-schism sequence. Reusing the stock impact sentence here makes the yard's conversion feel even more author-announced. REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6707-6710 with a concrete sign that Riva's line has finally reached the right listeners: a worker moving first, a tied child being physically repositioned, a skiff crew untying without looking at Daro again. The beat should be social and visible, not declared.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the collision of sanctity, lust, labor, rot, and family resentment. In edge cases, choose the more concrete, uncomfortable, and character-specific rendering over symbolic safety; if an image or action feels too polite for the pressure, it is probably wrong.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, violence, shame, grief, or economic pressure drives a scene, vague language counts as a failed beat; replace euphemism with the exact bodily, social, or material consequence the character experiences.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm across dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for ritual speech, emphasis, or deliberate character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative movement aggressively: follow ordeal with meal, revelation with labor, schism with care, and public spectacle with close-bodied aftermath so no adjacent chapters share the same structural rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, bodily, locally sacred, and unsanitized; lyrical only when pressure earns it, never as a decorative default.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person rotating among the five siblings; each scene stays inside one consciousness and lets the river press through perception rather than omniscient explanation.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate muscular propulsion with denser lyric accumulation; peak scenes should tighten and clarify rather than dissolve into fragmentary haze.
